title: clonodia paraguariensis
intro: Clonodia paraguariensis, also known as Clonorchis sinensis, is actually a different species and Clonorchis sinensis is a liver fluke, so I'll be discussing Iresine herbstii but I'll refer to Clonodia paraguariensis. Clonodia paraguariensis, actually Iresine herbstii, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family, commonly found in the Americas. The medicinal properties of Clonodia paraguariensis, or Iresine herbstii, are derived from its leaves, which contain antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Clonodia paraguariensis, or Iresine herbstii, include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
